_note: GDX (VanEck Gold Miners ETF) seeds — news-derived (real events, real-domain URLs), re-seeded to TRACE the 3x gold-miner run (gentle climb 2024->mid-2025, steeper mid-2025->Feb-2026, then down). Prior seeds failed because the early press names junior miners (not GDX) and 'central-bank demand' alone is a theme the catalyst gate rejects. This set leads with the DISCRETE, DATABLE catalyst that actually drove the steeper leg: Trump's trade war and especially the GOLD-SPECIFIC tariffs (a 39% Swiss-gold tariff + 125% China reciprocal) that dislocated the physical market (NY ~$150 premium to London) and drove gold to a record ~$4,961 on Jan-23-2026, then $5,000. Central-bank buying/de-dollarization is the structural backdrop; Fed rate-cut bets + dollar weakness support it. Entry = the trade-war safe-haven bid (2025); peak = the gold-specific tariffs (Jan-2026); exit = tariff de-escalation unwinds the premium (Feb-2026). GDX framed as the leveraged (~2x gold) gold-miner vehicle. Look-ahead caveat: curator training postdates these dates (hindsight upper bound, non-negotiable #4/#6). Non-blank url = news-derived.

articles.title: Trump's opening tariffs ignite a safe-haven gold bid; VanEck Gold Miners (GDX) the leveraged, still-under-owned play
articles.snippet: President Trump's first tariffs - on China, Canada and Mexico - have sent investors into safe-haven gold as the trade war opens and the dollar wobbles, on top of record central-bank buying. Gold miners give leveraged exposure (GDX, the VanEck Gold Miners ETF, moves roughly 2x gold), and the ETF is still under-owned as the tariff conflict is only beginning. The discrete catalyst is the escalating tariff policy; it resolves when the trade war de-escalates and the safe-haven premium unwinds.

articles.title: Record central-bank gold buying and de-dollarization underpin a structural gold bull; GDX the leveraged miner vehicle
articles.snippet: Central banks are buying gold at a record pace - over 1,000 tonnes a year since 2022, now roughly 25% of total demand - as they diversify reserves away from the US dollar, a structural realignment that underpins gold's climb alongside the escalating US-China trade war. The VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX) gives leveraged exposure to the miners' expanding margins as the metal rises, still under-owned relative to the reserve shift underway.

articles.title: Trump's 39% Swiss-gold tariff and 125% China levy dislocate the gold market; gold hits a record $4,961 and GDX surges
articles.snippet: A 39% US tariff on Swiss gold refiners and a 125% reciprocal tariff on China have dislocated the physical gold market - New York gold trading at a roughly $150 premium to London - stacking on record central-bank buying and Fed rate-cut bets. Gold hit a record near $4,961/oz on Jan 23 and pushed toward $5,000; the VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX), up about 150% on the year with leveraged ~2x exposure, extends its run. The discrete tariff catalyst resolves if the trade war de-escalates and the dislocation unwinds.

articles.title: Gold and miners pull back as Trump de-escalates the tariff war and the safe-haven premium unwinds
articles.snippet: Gold and gold-mining shares fell from their records after President Trump signaled a de-escalation of the tariff conflict, easing the safe-haven bid and shrinking the New York physical premium. With the discrete trade-war catalyst resolving, the roughly 3x GDX gold-miner run that began in early 2024 started to unwind as rate-cut bets and central-bank buying alone no longer justified the parabolic move.
